summary: A single large, multi-level rectangular-ish building with layered horizontal banding on walls, a stepped red brick/gable-like roof with a small square roof turret, white corner/column accents and a raised foundation; surrounded by a low white block border on a dirt plot.

shape.overall_form: rectangular main mass with a small projecting entrance/wing on one long side and a recessed courtyard/indentation on the opposite long side
shape.symmetry: approximately symmetrical along the long axis but with an offset entrance/wing on one side

elements: striped horizontal wall bands (alternating light and darker thin stripes)
stepped red brick roof with slope and ridge
small square rooftop turret/chimney with overhanging cap
white vertical corner/column supports projecting from walls
perimeter low white block fence/bollards around plot
rebuild_hints: Build a rectangular footprint ~28x20 with a small projecting wing on one long side and an inward notch on the opposite side as visible from top-down views.

Construct walls with repeating horizontal bands: light smooth block, thin darker stripe (wood or similar), repeated three times up the wall with quartz vertical columns at regular intervals and stone-like base.
Create a stepped gable-style roof using brick blocks with mirrored slopes on both long sides; add a small centered square turret on the ridge with an overhanging cap.
Place white single-block posts/bollards around the building perimeter on a dirt foundation matching the grid spacing seen in images.
uncertainties: Exact block types for the light wall bands: appears like smooth pale stone or sandstone; could be sandstone, smooth_sand, or white concrete.
The thin darker horizontal stripes appear wooden but may be a different brown block; exact material uncertain.
Window placement and glazing type are not clearly visible; glass presence is inferred but uncertain.
Precise internal floor count is inferred from exterior banding and roof height; could be 1 large interior with high ceilings rather than 2 conventional floors.
